agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive overview of Burnaby RCMP Community Safety Response Team
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will receive a verbal report from the RCMP providing an overview of the Community Safety Response Team (CSRT). This is an informational update only — no bylaw or policy change is being proposed at this time. The CSRT is a specialized unit that addresses complex public safety issues, such as mental health crises, drug-related incidents, and community disorder. Business owners should be aware of the team's role and how it may interact with their operations, but no immediate action is required.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
VERBAL REPORT: BURNABY RCMP - COMMUNITY SAFETY RESPONSE TEAM
Purpose: To provide Committee with an overview of the Burnaby RCMP Community Safety Response Team.
agenda_briefs.article_title: Public Safety Committee Meeting Agenda — April 23, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on 2026 Community Safety Plan update
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a progress report and 2026 update to the Burnaby Community Safety and Well-Being Plan. This is a proposed plan that outlines how the city aims to improve safety and well-being for residents and businesses. The update is not yet approved — Council will vote on whether to accept the report and move forward with the 2026 actions. Business owners should watch for how this could affect local safety programs, bylaw enforcement, or community services.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — this plan could shape future safety programs and bylaw changes that affect your business.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2
SU: BURNABY COMMUNITY SAFETY AND WELL-BEING PLAN PROGRESS REPORT AND 2026 UPDATE
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on the 2025 Community Safety and Well-Being Plan.
agenda_briefs.article_title: Public Safety Committee Meeting Agenda — April 23, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to review RCMP policing performance report
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update on RCMP policing operations for December 2025 to January 2026, along with the Annual Performance Plan (APP) quarterly report for October to December 2025. This is a routine update — no new rules or fees are proposed. The report covers crime trends, response times, and community safety initiatives in Burnaby. Business owners should be aware of local policing priorities that may affect their operations, but no immediate action is required.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.3
SU: RCMP STATUS REPORT (DECEMBER 2025 - JANUARY 2026) AND ANNUAL PERFORMANCE PLAN QUARTERLY REPORT (OCTOBER - DECEMBER 2025)
Purpose: To provide a status update (SU) on policing operations covering December 2025 to January 2026 and the Annual Performance Plan (APP) quarterly report covering October to December 2025.
agenda_briefs.article_title: Public Safety Committee Meeting Agenda — April 23, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Fire Department status update for February 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update from the Burnaby Fire Department covering operations through the end of February 2026. This is a routine report to keep council informed about fire department activities, response times, and any emerging issues. No new rules or fees are proposed in this update — it is an information item only.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4
SU: FIRE DEPARTMENT (FEBRUARY 2026)
Purpose: To provide the Burnaby Fire Department's status update (SU) available to the end of February 2026.
agenda_briefs.article_title: Public Safety Committee Meeting Agenda — April 23, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Community Safety Advisory Committee update
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a status update on the work of the Community Safety Advisory Committee (CSAC) for March-April 2026. This committee advises Council on local safety issues. The update is for information only — no bylaw or rule changes are proposed at this time. Business owners should watch for future recommendations that could affect safety requirements or operations.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.5
SU: COMMUNITY SAFETY ADVISORY COMMITTEE (MARCH - APRIL 2026)
Purpose:
To provide a status update (SU) on the work of Community Safety Advisory Committees (CSAC) for March - April 2026.
agenda_briefs.article_title: Public Safety Committee Meeting Agenda — April 23, 2026
